Company.business_summary: Alphabet Inc. offers various products and platforms in the United States, Europe, the Middle East, Africa, the Asia-Pacific, Canada, and Latin America. It operates through Google Services, Google Cloud, and Other Bets segments. The Google Services segment provides products and services, including ads, Android, Chrome, devices, Gmail, Google Drive, Google Maps, Google Photos, Google Play, Search, and YouTube. It is also involved in the sale of apps and in-app purchases and digital content in Google Play and YouTube; and devices, as well as the provision of YouTube consumer subscription services, such as YouTube TV, YouTube Music and Premium, NFL Sunday Ticket, and Google One. The Google Cloud segment offers consumption-based fees and subscriptions for AI solutions, including AI infrastructure, Vertex AI platform, and Gemini enterprise. It also provides cybersecurity, and data and analytics services; Google Workspace that include cloud-based communication and collaboration tools for enterprises, such as Calendar, Gmail, Docs, Drive, and Meet; and other enterprise services. The Other Bets segment sells transportation and internet services. Alphabet Inc. was incorporated in 1998 and is headquartered in Mountain View, California.

Netflix, Disney, YouTube target $2B FIFA rights package for next World Cups - CNBC
latest.summary: Investing.com -- A massive bidding war is brewing over the U.S. broadcast rights for the 2030 and 2034 FIFA World Cups, as streaming and legacy media giants look to sideline incumbent Fox Corp (NASDAQ:FOX). According to a recent report from CNBC, heavyweights Netflix Inc (NASDAQ:NFLX), Walt Disney Company (NYSE:DIS), and Alphabet’s (NASDAQ:GOOGL) YouTube are all actively exploring bids for the coveted soccer package. Discussions between FIFA and prospective media partners are slated to kick off

latest.title: Amazon Stocks Climbs As AI Push Drives $25 Billion Bond Sale
latest.summary: Amazon stock climbed Tuesday, following reports the tech giant is raising at least $25 billion through a bond offering to support its big investments into AI-related infrastructure.  The Seattle-based Amazon is raising the funds through an eight-part bond sale, according to reports in Bloomberg and the Wall Street Journal.  Amazon disclosed plans for the bond sale in regulatory filings Tuesday but did not disclose the amount.

latest.title: A Circuit Board Problem Just Delayed Nvidia’s Next AI System to 2028 And Chip Stocks Are Already Feeling the Fallout
latest.summary: A printed circuit board just pushed NVIDIA’s most powerful AI system into 2028 — at least according to one research firm. Semiconductor research firm SemiAnalysis reported on July 5–6, 2026 that NVIDIA’s Kyber NVL144 rack-scale AI system has been delayed, from its planned 2027 launch to 2028. CNBC cited the SemiAnalysis post. NVIDIA publicly disputed ... A Circuit Board Problem Just Delayed Nvidia’s Next AI System to 2028 And Chip Stocks Are Already Feeling the Fallout

latest.title: SpaceX Joins the Nasdaq-100. Why the Stock Dropped.
latest.summary: SpaceX  trading losses on Tuesday didn’t appear to have much to do with space fundamentals.  Elon Musk’s AI and space company entered the  one of the best-known large-cap growth indexes that tracks the largest nonfinancial companies listed on Nasdaq.  A stock pop was possible, as passive funds snap up shares.

latest.title: SpaceX Just Got 14 New Buy Ratings—and 1 Crazy Price Target
latest.summary: The wait is over for  SpaceX  Shares have picked up a bevy of new ratings following the company’s IPO in June.  Wall Street is very bullish on Elon Musk’s rocket and artificial-intelligence company—especially Raymond James.  The most surprising target comes from Raymond James’ Brian Gesuale.

latest.title: DeepSeek looks to reduce reliance on Nvidia with own AI chip
latest.summary: Yahoo Finance's Dan Howley joins Josh Lipton to examine DeepSeek's development of a custom AI chip to bypass U.S. export restrictions and reduce its reliance on Nvidia.

latest.title: Google Co-Founder Worth $280 Billion Flees NYC Real Estate at 94% Loss Ahead of Mamdani’s Rent Freeze
latest.summary: The world’s third-richest person just accepted a 94% loss to walk away from New York City real estate. Google co-founder Sergey Brin, worth roughly $280 billion, sold his stake in a Manhattan multifamily fund for six cents on the dollar, according to Bloomberg. The direction of that trade matters more than the dollar figure to ... Google Co-Founder Worth $280 Billion Flees NYC Real Estate at 94% Loss Ahead of Mamdani’s Rent Freeze

latest.title: Amazon Plans $25 Billion Bond Sale
latest.summary: The debt offering comes as large tech companies keep funding AI and data-center expansion.

latest.title: Who will be the AI trade's real winners: The makers vs. the takers?
latest.summary: Samsung Electronics (005930.KS) released preliminary second quarter earnings results on Tuesday, reporting a 19-fold profit jump from a year ago, amounting to $58 billion. Morning Brief Host Julie Hyman welcomes Yahoo Finance Senior Business Reporter Ines Ferré and Interactive Brokers chief strategist Steve Sosnick to help break down whether Samsung's breakout numbers still weren't enough to impress Wall Street investors and what it signifies for the broader chip space.
